text: Carbohelicenes are well-known chiral polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (CPAHs) with a helical structure that often exhibit interesting chiroptical properties including circularly polarized luminescence (CPL). Synthesis of larger enantiomerically-enriched helicene derivatives including their fused analogues have been reported in literature. To the best of our knowledge, direct catalytic synthesis of lower non-fused carbo[n]helicenes (n = 4–6) has not been reported so far. We here present palladium(0) catalyzed C–H arylation to produce lower carbohelicenes efficiently. With the chiral bifunctional phosphine-carboxylate ligand is shown to catalyze the C–H arylation reaction of achiral precursors to yield the corresponding carbohelicenes with e.r. up to 98:2. The efficient synthesis of various lower carbohelicenes including carbo[4]-, [5]-, and [6]helicenes and azahelicene derivatives has been reported. DFT calculation revealed that the catalytic stereochemistry is controlled by the C–H activation/reductive elimination process which is in turn highly dependent on the non-covalent interactions between the ligand and the substrate and the steric interactions between them. The photophysical and chiroptical studies were carried out in detail and it is observed that the dissymmetry factor of the smaller congeners like carbo[4]helicenes are quite large which opens up new avenues for designing chiroptical materials.
